search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae atopic dermatitis telehealth guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: contact-dermatitis guidance as complete atopic-eczema coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Contact and atopic dermatitis are distinct contexts.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for child and adult atopic dermatitis; not exact for every allergic, irritant, seborrhoeic, stasis, or other eczema subtype.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person infection assessment and selected testing.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 document, due for revision 2029-02-21, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA atopic-dermatitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped atopic-dermatitis terminology, itch, dry skin, flare, atopy, allergy, sleep, distribution, and morphology fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fever, discharge or infection concern, rapid worsening, severe flare, sleep, face or eye, and return fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped morphology, distribution, atopy, prior history, and selected clinician-reviewed assessment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented emollient, skin care, trigger, medicine, flare-plan, education, question, and follow-up fields without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Allergic dermatitis as a separate diagnosis, discharge-specific culture review, immunocompromise, mucosal or wound fields, fixed two-to-four-week review timing, and dose-bearing medicines remain unsupported.
Atopic dermatitis remains clinician-stated and severity or infection must not be inferred automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
